data.title: Super League Shares Climb After Final Private Placement
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Super League Enterprise shares rose after it said it closed the second and final tranche of its private placement financing.

The stock gained 11% to $2.38 in after-hours trading on Friday. Through the close, shares had lost 91% of their value this year.

The Santa Monica, Calif., media company has secured $17.9 million in total gross proceeds from new and existing investors, it said. The offering was anchored by the previously announced $10 million investment from Evo Fund and added $2.65 million in the final tranche.

Super League will emerge with more than $15 million of deployable capital after accounting for transaction fees, expenses and debt repayments. Management believes that funding will be enough to cover its operations for the foreseeable future.

The financing is the culmination of a six-month turnaround plan to eliminate all outstanding debt by next month, streamline capital structure and gain enough capital to fund operations.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Health Care Up After HCA, Sanofi Earnings — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked up after a generally strong round of earnings.

Hospital chain HCA Healthcare boosted its full-year growth outlook after logging higher profit and revenue in the third quarter, as same-facility admissions grew.

French drug maker Sanofi shares rose after it said a jump in sales of its blockbuster Dupixent anti-inflammatory drug and robust pharmaceutical launches drove higher revenue and adjusted earnings for the third quarter. Sanofi said it remained in talks with the U.S. government over drug pricing.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Consumer Cos Up After Inflation Data — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rallied following muted inflation data.

The Consumer Price Index rose 0.3% in September, which was cooler than the 0.4% rate economists had projected. The data indicate less pressure on U.S. shoppers ahead of the key holiday shopping season, and also open the door for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

The University of Michigan's consumer-sentiment index for October fell to 53.6 from an initial October reading of 55 published two weeks ago and 55.1 last month.

Alaska Air Group said that more than 360 flights had been canceled after a technology outage disrupted its operations for hours late Thursday, prompting the carrier to delay its earnings call.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Starcore Announces Results of Annual General Meeting
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - October 24, 2025) - Starcore International Mines Ltd. ("Starcore" or the "Company") reports the results of its annual general meeting of shareholders (the "Meeting") held on October 24, 2025, at which 48.75% of shareholders entitled to vote were present.

Shareholders elected all directors nominated by management, as follows:

DirectorPercentage of Votes For
Gary Arca85.44%
Robert Eadie85.44%
Jordan Estra99.94%
Salvador Garcia85.44%
Federico Villaseñor85.52%

All other matters presented at the Meeting received over 96% approval from the shareholders at the meeting, including the appointment of Baker Tilly WM LLP, Chartered Professional Accountants, as auditors of Starcore, and more importantly:

  • the Plan of Arrangement covering the spin-out of the Company's mineral property interests in Côte d'Ivoire; and
  • the private placement offering to raise up to $5,000,000 through the distribution of up to 20 million units at $0.25 per unit.

Subsequent to the Meeting, the Board of Directors appointed Robert Eadie as President and Chief Executive Officer, Salvador Garcia as Chief Operating Officer, Gary Arca as Chief Financial Officer and Cynthia Avelino as Corporate Secretary.

About Starcore

Starcore International Mines is engaged in precious metals production with focus and experience in Mexico. While this base of producing assets is complemented by exploration and development projects throughout North America, Starcore has expanded its reach internationally with the project in Côte d'Ivoire. The Company is a leader in Corporate Social Responsibility and advocates value driven decisions that will increase long term shareholder value. You can find more information on the investor friendly website at www.starcore.com.

ON BEHALF OF STARCORE INTERNATIONAL

MINES LTD.

(Signed) "Robert Eadie"

Robert Eadie, President & Chief Executive Officer

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASE CONTACT:

ROBERT EADIE

Telephone: (604) 602-4935

LinkedIn

X

Facebook

The Toronto Stock Exchange has not reviewed nor does it accept responsibility

for the adequacy or accuracy of this press release.

NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION IN THE UNITED STATES

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/271926

data.title: TELEDYNE TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Teledyne Technologies Inc., a leading provider of sophisticated instrumentation, digital imaging products, and aerospace and defense electronics, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market dynamics.

Financial Highlights

Teledyne Technologies Inc. reported robust financial performance for the third quarter of 2025, driven by strategic acquisitions and strong market demand across its business segments.

  • Net Sales: $1,539.5 million, an increase of 6.7% from the third quarter of 2024, primarily due to sales from recent acquisitions in the Aerospace and Defense Electronics segment.
  • Cost of Sales: $880.0 million, up 6.8% from the third quarter of 2024, driven by higher net sales.
  • Operating Income: $282.8 million, a 4.5% increase from the third quarter of 2024, reflecting higher operating income in the Instrumentation and Aerospace and Defense Electronics segments.
  • Net Income Attributable to Teledyne: $220.7 million, a decrease of 15.8% from the third quarter of 2024, primarily due to the favorable resolution of an uncertain tax position in the prior year.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $4.65, compared with $5.54 in the third quarter of 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income attributable to Teledyne.

Business Highlights

Teledyne Technologies Inc. demonstrated strong operational performance across its various business segments, supported by strategic acquisitions and new product launches.

  • Revenue Segments: The Digital Imaging segment saw increased sales primarily due to higher sales of commercial infrared imaging components and subsystems, unmanned air systems, and industrial automation imaging systems. The Instrumentation segment experienced growth driven by higher sales in marine and environmental instrumentation.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains the largest market, contributing significantly to net sales across all segments. Europe and Asia also showed strong performance, with notable sales in the Digital Imaging and Instrumentation segments.
  • Sales Units: The Aerospace and Defense Electronics segment benefited from recent acquisitions, contributing to a significant increase in sales units, particularly in defense electronics.
  • New Product Launches: The company launched new products in the unmanned air systems category, contributing to increased sales in the Digital Imaging segment.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in the Aerospace and Defense Electronics segment due to recent acquisitions and expects to leverage synergies across its business segments to drive operational efficiency and market expansion.

SEC Filing: TELEDYNE TECHNOLOGIES INC [ TDY ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: Financials Up as Credit, Inflation Fears Subside — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose sharply as credit and inflation fears subsided.

Wall Street deal powerhouse Goldman Sachs Group rose by 4.4%.

Zelle, the payments product owned by the country's largest banks, said it plans to allow users to start making international payments using stablecoins, a development that draws the big banks backing the payment technology into the business of cryptocurrency transfers for the first time.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Procter & Gamble Expects $1.5 Billion–$2 Billion in Pre-Tax Restructuring Costs Over Two Years – SEC Filing
data.description:
Procter & Gamble Co :

  • P&G - SEES TO INCUR ABOUT $1.5-$2 BILLION IN BEFORE-TAX RESTRUCTURING COSTS OVER TWO YEARS - SEC FILING

data.title: Microsoft's Xbox to Remake Original Halo Video Game
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Microsoft's Xbox is remaking the original Halo video game with plans to support multiplayer gaming across several consoles for the first time.

The remake, titled Halo: Campaign Evolved, will support the game's original two-player split screen experience on console as well as allow up to four players across Xbox, PC and PlayStation 5, according to a blog post on Xbox's website.

The game will include new visuals and story content, remastered music and re-recorded voice lines. It will also bring back some weapons and vehicles from later Halo titles, Xbox said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: FirstService Raised to Buy From Hold by TD Securities
data.description: (21:29 GMT) FirstService Price Target Cut to $213.00/Share From $214.00 by TD Securities
